steps.title: Track the passage structure
steps.content: The passage first reports what researchers found about Matabele ants: they can identify infected wounds, treat them with antimicrobial secretions, and greatly reduce mortality among ants with infected injuries. Those details are research findings, not a description of a problem or a list of experimental steps.

steps.title: Reject missing structure elements
steps.content:
• Choice B: The passage introduces research, but it does not explain research methods or procedures.
  • Choice C: The passage describes ant behavior, but it does not speculate about how that behavior evolved.
  • Choice D: The passage does not frame the ants' behavior as an issue that needs a proposed solution.

steps.title: Use findings and future use
steps.content: After summarizing the findings, the final sentence shifts to what the discovery could make possible: future development of new antibiotics for humans. So the structure moves from research findings to an area for further research. The answer is A.
